name: Hungarian Tarts
recipeIngredient: 7.5 oz Dry Farmer's cheese or Ricotta
recipeInstructions:
Add until smooth and of spreading consistency. If you cannot obtain really dry cottage cheese (I can't here anymore); I have used Ricotta (only the best grade). Omit the sour cream as the ricotta is not as dry as the Farmer's Cheese; dry cheese is preferable.
- ALMOND: Mix together for ALMOND FILLING
- PECAN FILLING: Mix together for Pecan filling
- WALNUT FILLING: Mix all together, cool and spread or fill.
- KIFLI-Hungarian filled cookies: Mix well, form into one-inch balls; place in refrigerator overnight. The next day, roll thin, individually, fill with walnut, apricot, or poppyseed filling. Bake 10 minutes at 400°F. Brush with beaten egg white and sprinkle with sugar.
- Walnut filling can be used in Nut Rolls, Kiflis or Tarts.
- Tart recipe to follow, uses almond, coconut, pecan, or walnut fillings. Mix well and shape into half-inch balls. Refrigerate overnight and press into 1-inch tart pans and fill as follows:
- Almond tarts: Put 1/2 tsp. pineapple preserves in the bottom of the tart, almond paste to 3/4 full; bake 15-20 minutes at 350°F.
- Pecan tarts: Put 1/2 pecan in the bottom of the tart and fill 3/4 full with pecan filling; bake 15-20 minutes at 350°F.
- Walnut tarts: Fill 3/4 full of walnut paste and bake 15-20 minutes at 350°F.
- COCONUT RECIPE ON THE NEXT PAGE. Put jelly in the bottom of the tart. Fill 3/4 full with coconut filling. Bake for half an hour at 350°F.
